text: Protein source (animal vs. plant) does not significantly affect changes in absolute lean mass when total protein intake is above the recommended dietary allowance.
Protein source (animal vs. plant) does not significantly affect changes in muscle strength when total protein intake is adequate.
Animal protein has a small favoring effect over plant protein on percent lean mass (proportion of body weight that is lean tissue).
Younger adults (under 50 years) gain more absolute lean mass with animal protein compared to plant protein, with a weighted mean difference of 0.41 kg.
Younger adults (under 50 years) gain more percent lean mass with animal protein compared to plant protein, with a weighted mean difference of 0.50%.
The addition of resistance exercise training does not change the relative effect of animal vs. plant protein on lean mass or muscle strength outcomes.
